input.new_string:
if not self.image:
            return self

        self.get_image_mimetype()

        self.image = base64.b64encode(self.image)

        return self

    def get_image_mimetype(self) -> str | None:
        """Return the image mimetype, guessing and caching it lazily if not already set.

        The guess is only performed against `self.image` bytes (never `path` or `url`)
        to avoid triggering resource-intense operations.
        """
        if not self.image_mimetype and self.image:
            guess = filetype.guess(self.image)
            self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None

        return self.image_mimetype

    def resolve_image(self, as_base64: bool = False) -> BytesIO:

from llama_index.core.base.llms.types import ImageBlock

ib = ImageBlock(image=png)
print('mimetype:', ib.get_image_mimetype())
print('cached same:', ib.get_image_mimetype() is ib.image_mimetype)
"

ib = ImageBlock(image=png)

ib2 = ImageBlock(image=b'notanimage')
print('no guess:', ib2.get_image_mimetype())

content.text: Implemented `get_image_mimetype()` on `ImageBlock` in `llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py`: it lazily guesses and caches `image_mimetype` from `self.image` bytes, and `image_to_base64` now delegates to it instead of inlining the guess logic.
